desc:
Here the trail curves gently through the landscape, it goes west and comes from
the south. Smoothly rounded foothills can be seen in the east and the smoke from
some fire is hanging in the air here. The smell of meat and wood suggests that
someone is smoking freshly caught game and the scents seems to be coming from a
building that can be seen to the southwest.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
You see a cabin to your west and a barrel of water stands in front of it. There
is smoke coming from the chimney, and the air smells of freshly cooked meat. The
cabin is made of thick logs and has two small windows and a sturdy oakdoor.
Several dead rabbits are hung to dry in the sun on a wooden rack. The trail you
are standing on leads on north and east of here.
name: A Trail Going Past a Cabin

desc:
The trail bends here, going west and south. Further west you can see smoke
coming from a small cabin, and to the north a small hill is visible. As a bird
flies off to the east you see foothills in that direction. A multitude of tracks
cover the trail, making individual tracks harder to distinguish.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail leaves north and east. It seems often travelled, although not often by
large creatures or wagons. The area west of here is filled with small rises and
inclines in the landscape. Another curve in the trail lies east of here.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail makes a sharp turn in the landscape here, turning south and west from
the point you are standing. It continues west for a while and then bends again,
and to the east some hills can be seen. Looking south you see that the trail
bends around another corner and vanishes from sight. No trees grow here, only
small bushes.
name: A Trail Going West and South

desc:
Looking north and east you see the trail heading onwards in those directions. A
hilly terrain streches out to the west and to the south some bushy fields can be
seen. Far to the north the trail bends again and east you notice a crossway far
away.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail comes to a crossing here, leaving in three directions. West, south and
east the trail leads. To the north it twists through some hilly terrain. Looking
further away in any direction yields little more information about the area, the
only thing you notice is that the trail goes straight south. A small sign has
been placed here.
name: A Crossing in the Trail

desc:
The trail bends in a nice curve going north and west. Looking further north you
notice that the trail bends again after a while and to the west the trail comes
to a crossway. A deep forest surrounds the trail to the east and south, the
forest seems to be very deep and also calm.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
A forest lies to the east, different animals can be seen and heard in the deep
but calm forest. The trail itself goes north and south here, looking further
south you see that it bends to the east and to the north it comes to a crossway
of some sort. Westward several bushes grow on a plain.
name: Upon a Winding Trail

desc:
Yet again the trail bends here on it way into the wilderness. It goes north and
east here and to the south plains covered with bushes stretch out. Looking west
you see more plains. The air is calm and not much wind ever disturbs this area.
Small rocks lie beside and on the road.
name: A Bend in the Winding Trail

desc:
The forest surrounds the western and nothern border of this area. Sounds from
different animals echoes out from the deep forest. The trail itself turns west
and south here. Many small curves in the trail makes it a little bit tiring to
walk on, and the small pebbles that lie on the trail makes it even harder to
walk without slipping a little.
name: A Bend in the Winding Trail

desc:
A curvy trail carves through the landscape here, going north and south. It is
bordered to the east by a deep forest with tall and ancient looking trees. The
trail is narrow and curves madly on its way into the wilderness. To the west a
vast bush-covered area stretches out.
name: A Winding Trail

desc:
The trails makes a bend to the north here, coming from the east. Looking west
you see some plains stretching out and to the south similar terrain can be seen.
A dark and calm looking forest is visible to the northeast and the trail goes on
for as far as you can see to the north.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail bends west and south here, while to the north a large forest is
visible. It seems to be very deep and different bird calls echoes out from it.
To the east you notice a large winegrape field that stretches out for as far as
you can see. Pebbles litter the ground here, making it tricky to walk without
slipping.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The curvy trail makes yet another bend on its way into the wilderness. Far to
the southeast you know that Mithlond is located, home of Cirdan and the fair
elves. In this area you see however, a river to the south that seems to be
rather rapid. The trail itself goes north and east here and looking west you see
some fields stretching out.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
Curving its way through the landscape the trail makes another bend here. This
time it turns west and south. Looking north you see a well-tended winegrape
field and beyond that you see the dark outline of a forest. Southward you hear
the noise of running water.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The river runs west to the west of here, but to the east the trail continues for
a while. Northward the trail also goes on in almost a straight line. Looking
south you notice the river again, looking even wilder than to the west. Bushes
and small trees grow along the trail.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail comes from the south and turns to the west here. A river can be heard
far to the west while to the east you see fields stretching out. Directly north
of the path you see winegrapes growing in straight rows, someone is caring well
for them.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail makes a sudden turn here, from the east to the north. The river can be
seen flowing far to the south, until you can just make out where it enters the
Gulf of Lhun. The southern most slopes of Ered Luin can be seen far to the
north.
name: A Dusty Trail

desc:
You are on a small dirt trail running north and south, along a rushing river.
Just to the south you see can see Mithlond. Far to the north you can make out
the southern peaks of Ered Luin. Beyond Mithlond you see the Gulf of Lhun
extending to the horizon.
name: A Dusty Trail

desc:
A small trail runs through the great fields north of Mithlond here. The path
continues to the north, and a rushing river runs to the west of the trail,
making its way to the Gulf of Lhun a few miles to the south. To the immediate
south are the North Gates of Forlond. The great, white stone walls that surround
the city break only here, and in that gap stands a huge, iron gate.
name: Before the North Gate of Forlond

desc:
The small trail bends here, going east and south. Looking west you notice a
rocky area, covered with sharp stones and boulders. To the east the trails
continues for a little bit, at least according to what you can see. A small
slope can be seen north.
name: A Trail Bending East and South

desc:
You have reached a crossing in the trail. The main trail leads north and west
here, while a small path to the east, covered with weeds and grass, seems to
lead across the bushy fields. South of you a large forest looms, its underbrush
looking rather dense and thorny. A faint sound of some small animal can be heard
in the distant.
name: A Crossway in the Trail

desc:
The trail goes north for as far as you can see, but to the south it bends in a
westward direction. Foothills are visible to your west, possibly containing some
small game. The grassy fields east of here seems like a peaceful place with the
tall grass gently swaying in the wind.
name: A Trail Leading North

desc:
The trail leads south from here. Small foothills lie a small distance to the
west from here, while some plains stretch out to the east. The trail seems to be
well trodden upon. Looking south you can see how the trail cuts through the
landscape in a snakelike manner.
name: A Trail

desc:
The trail is cleared from brush and other plants, and it looks to be in a decent
shape. Both sides are covered with dead trees and bushes. The landscape is
turning hilly to the northwest. The hills to the west are still very small and
can easily be travelled without any major hazards.
name: Trail in the Hills

desc:
The trail rambles along in a north-south direction, almost hidden away within
the foothills of this area. Smaller hills make the way west from here a bit
harder to travel without mounts. A small stone on the side of the trail has some
markings on it.
name: Trail in the Hills

desc:
Large hills cover the land both to the north and west. The uneven terrain
hinders the view of the surrounding land. Two oaks hang over the trail,
attempting to embrace and forming an arch above the trail heading to the north.
The path continues to the south where the land seems to be more even.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
A large hedge of blueberry bushes grows along the southern side of the trail.
Birds and other animals seem to have made off with most of the fruit. The trail
continues north along the broken hills of limestone to the northwest, and
travels along the uneven ground leading into them to the west. Only a few trees
can be seen along the top ridge of the hills.
name: Rambling Trail

desc:
The river Farnen flows into the Gulf of Lhun, forming a small delta. The river
flows from the north. To the east you can see Mithlond upon the north shores of
the Lhun. To the west you can see a small trail, and beyond the trail you can
see a large field.
name: River Delta

desc:
The river Farnen runs north and south here. The river rushes past you, and you
can hear and see large rapids. Huge rocks jut out from the shallow river bed. To
the south a ways you can see where the river runs into the Gulf of Lhun. To the
west you can see a small trail running along the river, and to the east you can
see the northern gates of Mithlond.
name: The River Farnen

desc:
The rushing river Farnen makes a turn from the west towards the Gulf of Lhun to
the south. Portions of a small trail can be seen to the north and east. Large
rocks make large rapids, and the shallow bottom makes the rapids even worse.
name: The River Farnen

desc:
The River Farnen curves from the north to the east here. Very large rocks on the
river bed make extremely large rapids, and the very shallow river makes the
rapids worse. To the north east you see a small dirt trail. To the south you see
another trail, and another portion of the trail can be seen to the west.
name: The River Farnen

desc:
The River Farnen makes a turn from the west to the south here. Large rocks on
the river bed make large rapids, and the shallow water makes the rapids even
worse. To the north you see a small dirt path, and another portion of the trail
can be seen to the east.
name: Bend in the River Farnen

desc:
The river suddenly turns from the north to the east here. To the west you see a
dirt path which follows the river's west bank. Large, sharp rocks jut out of the
riverbed, making large rapids. The river is rather shallow looking here, making
the rapids even worse. The river floor is devoid of plants, perhaps the river
flows too swiftly for any to grow here.
name: Bend in the River Farnen

desc:
Here the river makes a turn and it slowly picks up speed on its way south.
Looking north you see a track and east of you a different part of the same path
can be seen. South of you the river runs wild and water sprays at you from the
rapids. Large sharp rocks jut out of the riverbed here too.
name: Bend in the River Farnen

desc:
Going east and coming from the west, the river makes its way through the
landscape. It is calm and serene here and you doubt that the river flows wildly
even when it rains heavily further upstreams. Many boulders are visible in the
riverbed and fish can be seen darting in and out of hiding places. Looking north
you see a track and likewise south of you a track is visible.
name: River Farnen

desc:
The river comes from the north and flows eastward here. It is calm and a few
boulders can be spotted in the riverbed here. Looking west you spot a track
going further west and to the south you see the same track. Vegetation on the
riverbanks is sparse and you can not see any trees there.
name: Bend in River Farnen

desc:
Here the river turns south and west. Water flows from the west and slowly makes
its way southward. No current can be seen and the river seems to be rather calm.
North and east you spot plains stretching out and far east a track can be seen
going north.
name: Bend in River Farnen

desc:
The river flows calmy through this place and the riverbed is sandy. Clear water
allows you to see all the way to the bottom and you spot fish here and there.
Looking north you see forestland and south a path can be spotted.
name: River Farnen

desc:
A little calmer here, the river goes east and west, turning leisurely among
large rocks and boulders. Fishing might be good here and the wind is cut off
from this area since it is going through a small canyon here. Forest can be seen
north and southward a path goes west and east.
name: River Farnen

desc:
Water runs amongst the rocks here. At first sight the river seems to be calm
here but when you look closer you notice that the current is fast and several
whirls assure you of this. The river goes west and east and a forest covers the
northern riverbank.
name: River Farnen

desc:
Walking along the path you notice it goes on west and east. Looking north you
notice the river Farnen flowing slowly through the landscape. Further north you
see a forest but you cant see beyond that. Bushes grow beside the path and
travelling is easy.
name: Along a Tricky Path

desc:
The path goes on both east and west and travelling is not hard at all here.
Looking north you see the river Farnen flowing and beyond the river you see a
forest stretching out. Several bushes grow here but no tress cover the sides of
the path.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The small path turns south and west here and looking both north and east you see
a river rushing along. The river looks rather wild to the east but the north
section seems to be calm. Beyond the river you notice a forest stretching out.
Bushes grow beside the path here.
name: A Bend in the Tricky Path

desc:
You are in a large field. The grass grows knee high in most places, and the
sounds of insects and birds is overwelming. To the south, a long ways off, you
can see the Gulf of Lhun. You can make out the southern slopes of Ered Luin a
few miles to the north.
name: Field

desc:
This small path turns from the north to the south here. To the north and east
you see a river rushing along. To the west and south you see a great field
extending off, until far away it meets the shores of the Lhun. Across the river,
you can make out a trail, and more of the fields. Far to the north, you can see
southernmost slopes of Ered Luin.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The grass in this field is knee high in most places. It goes on in all
directions. Far to the south you can see where it meets the north shore of the
Gulf of Lhun. Many miles to the north you see the southernmost slopes of Ered
Luin.
name: Fields

desc:
This immense field extends to the south and west, and a part of a small trail
can be seen to the north and east. Just beyond the trail you see a rushing
river. Far to the south you see the shores of the Gulf of Lhun. The grass in
this field is teeming with life, and most of the grass grows up to your knees.
name: Fields

desc:
The path turns from the east to the north here. To the northeast you see a river
making its way to the Gulf of Lhun. Away to the north you can see the peaks of
Ered Luin. In all other directions immense fields extends for several miles. Far
to the south you can make out the northern shores of the Lhun.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
This field is simply huge. It extends for quite a ways to the south and west. A
small trail can be seen to the north, and another portion of it can be seen to
the east. To the south a ways you can see the Gulf of Lhun. Far away to the
north you can see Ered Luin.
name: Fields

desc:
The trail turns for the north to west here. On the north-west side of the path
you can see a rushing river. On the other side of the trail a great field
extends to the southwest, until it meets the Gulf of Lhun, a little ways to the
south. To the southeast you can see Mithlond. Far to the north you can see the
high peaks of Ered Luin.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
A small trail runs through the huge grassland here. The trail turns from the
west to the south here, and to the north you can see a large river running, and
another portion of the river can be seen to the east. Far to the south you can
see the shores of the Gulf of Lhun. Between the shores and the path there is a
massive grassland. To the southeast you can make out Mithlond.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The large fields to the north come to a sudden end here, at the Gulf of Lhun. To
the east a large river enters the gulf, and to the west you can see the shores
continue. To the north all you can see is the massive fields extending on into
the horizon.
name: Fields

desc:
The small trail comes to a sudden end here, at the shores of the Gulf of Lhun.
The trail goes on to the north, while to the west and north you an immense field
can be seen. To the east a large river flows into the Lhun. The smell of the
salty ocean is thick here, and the ocean extends into the horizon to the south.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The path turns from the west to the south here. To the east and north you see a
rushing river making its way to the Gulf of Lhun. On the other side of the
trail, opposite the river, you see a massive field extending off into the
distance. Far to the north you see the southern slopes of Ered Luin.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The river north of here suddenly turns south here, forcing the trail to bend.
Off in the distance to the north you see the slopes of Ered Luin, and to the
south very far away you see the shores of the Lhun.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
This path leads east and west here, along the shore of a rushing river, which
lies to the north. To the south, you can see a massive field extending off into
the distance, until it meets the shores of the Lhun. Looking across the river,
you can see another trail and more of the plains. To the north, off in the
distance, you see the southern slopes of Ered Luin.
name: On a Tricky Path

desc:
The trail runs both to the north and east from this point. To the west, the
hills slowly become larger. Off to the south the land turns into rolling
foothills. Only a few animal tracks can be seen and a careful watcher might spot
the occasional small rodent.
name: Foothills by the Trail

desc:
The hills are smaller here than to the west and looking east you see a trail far
in the distance. Small bushes and shrubberies litter the surroundings and
several small pebbles lie here.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Hills stretch out west and south but to the north you notice a track going north
and looking east you see the same track. Travelling here is not hard but the
tracks look more inviting. A large tree also stands here, it is old and
withered, many broken branches cover it sides.
name: Small Hills

desc:
A large number of rocks and boulders are lying on the ground. Many are sharp and
walking is made difficult because of them. A few bushes and trees grow here but
mostly the vegetation is sparse. To the north you see more hills and eastward
some bushy land is visible. To the west however, large hills conquer the horizon
and southward is more bushy land.
name: Rocky Foothills

desc:
Many large hills is what makes up this area. A few trees grow on the slopes and
the wildlife seems to be rich. Boulders of all sizes are strewn throughout the
landscape and moss covers them. The air is usually not still here and it gets a
bit cold if the sun is down.
name: King Hills

desc:
Many and most of them thorned bushes grow here, a few trees also grows here but
they are small and obviously not doing well. A trail is visible to the east and
north, while to the south the bushes continue to grow for as far as you can see.
Many animals can hide in this vegetation and you see tracks of some of them.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
Looking around you you notice that the same kind of terrain stretch out in all
directions except to the east where a trail is clearly visible. Bushes and small
trees make up the vegetation and many of the bushes seem to be some form of
fruit carrying bushes. Animals certainly enjoy this area but travelling is a
little hard due to the dense growth.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
Many bushes grow here making out a very different landscape compared to the
forest that lies to the south. Animals have left their tracks and traces here
and there, suggesting that the animal life here is rather rich. With exception
to the south, all other directions show wildbush country stretching out. The
wind here can be strong but the weather is usually calm.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
The forest ends here, it goes on the the west but the the east and north you see
plains and fields stretch out. A river flows through the landscape to the south
but you can not make out if it is calm or rapid there. The forest itself is lush
as ever and many animals might live here, taking advantage of the abundancy of
food in the area.
name: End of Lush Forest

desc:
Here the river flows past some fields. To the west a forest grows and to the
south the river is. A trail is visible to the east and north. Many small plants
grow here and it is evident that the area is good for certain berry carrying
bushes. You notice several growing here and you might find some to pluck if you
look.
name: Fields by the River

desc:
The bushy landscape here goes onward to the east. To the north east a trail can
be seen. South of here the bushes grow less dense and more trees are visible.
The bushes are growing very dense in some parts and animals seem to have made
them their homes.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
Bushes of all sizes and shapes grow here and grass covers the ground. Most of
the bushes have thorns but not all of them. A few small trees has managed to
survive here but they seem to be a bit unhealthy and not doing well. A forest
spreads out to the south and west while the bushy terrain continues north and
east.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
Looking east you see more of the same kind of landscape as here, a very bushy
field. To the north and west you notice some hills dominating the landscape but
to the south the bushes grow yet again. It is a good landscape for smaller
animals to live in and looking at the traces made by them you notice that the
animal life is very rich.
name: Wildbush Fields

desc:
To the north you see some majestic hills blocking the horizon while to the east
the landscape is similar to what it is here. There are rocks and boulders
everywhere and most are sharp and jagged. The vegetation here is mainly bushes
but some trees also manage to survive here. Looking south you see a lush forest
growing and eastwards you see a very bushy landscape.
name: Rocky Foothills

desc:
Lush and dense forest grows here, it is a perfect place for many plants to grow
and they are certainly growing. Animals must also find this a nice place to live
and breed. The thickness of the forest makes it hard to see what lies beyond a
few steps, but looking north you notice that the forest is thinning and some
hills take over.
name: Lush Forest

desc:
The trees here are very lush and nothing seems to mind you being here. Only the
angry birds that cry out from the trees might have hostile feelings to- wards
you. The forest seems to go on in all direction except to the south where a
river flows calmly through the landscape. The air is fresh and filled with
different kinds of scents from the trees.
name: In the Middle of a Lush Forest

desc:
Forest with several kinds of different trees grows here. The trees are lush and
seems to be very healthy, with a river to the south they most certainly never
suffer from droughts. Animals have made this forest their home and though it
seems to be calm here there might be someone watching you.
name: Lush Forest

desc:
Standing here you see a river running around you from the west to the south, it
flows smoothly for most parts as far as you can see. North of you and east, you
notice a curvy trail going into the wilderness. Different bushes also grows here
on the riverbank, offering shelter to different small animals.
name: Fields by the River

desc:
The vineyard stretches out as far as you can see north of here but far west you
can see a trail going through the landscape. South and east of here some plains
or fields stretch out. The grapes have been grown in orderly rows and the soil
seems well tended and the air has a sweet scent to it.
name: Vineyard

desc:
Vines cover this area, growing in straight rows. To the west and south the
vineyard continues while to the east plains stretch out. North of you a strange
bush blocks your path, it grows dense and has sharp thorns. The grapes seem to
be well taken care for and the soil is well turned.
name: Vineyard

desc:
Here you stand in front of a hut, looking out at the fields that stretch out for
as far as you can see in all directions. Vines grow in neat and orderly rows
here that stretch out southward. Far to the northwest you notice a deep and calm
looking forest.
name: Vineyard

desc:
Standing in the middle of a large vineyard, you see a trail to your west and
south, whilst in all other directions the fields stretch out. Very far to the
north you can see a building of some sort and to the northwest you see the
ending of the fields and the start of a forest.
name: Vineyard

desc:
The vines have grown here for a long time. Climate and the right amount of
humidity allows them to grow most of the year. They have been planted in neat
and orderly rows and seem to be very well cared for. You might find grapes on
the stalks to enjoy if you look. North of here is a forest that protects the
fields from cold winds from the north. Westward you see a curvy trail going
northward. A hut can be seen to the northeast.
name: Vineyard

desc:
Looking south of here you see well tended winestalks growing and north of here
the forest makes it hard to see what is there, the trees are simply growing too
dense there. Same with the western part of the forest. You can't go east because
fallen trees and wildly sprawling, thorny bushes block your way. Many noises can
be heard echoing around you and the air is soft and humid. Not even a strong
wind would make itself noticeable in here since the trees form a wall to protect
you from it.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
A large area of thorny bushes stretch out south from here. It is impossible to
hack a way through the bushes, they grow dense and have thin branches that only
bend when you swing at them. You can however walk in any other direction, to the
north and west more grassy fields are visible and east a plain of some sort
lies. The air is still and a little bit humid here, several small animals can be
heard rummaging in the bushes.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
A forest is growing to the west, it is very deep and calm. To the north and east
more fields stretch out but to the south a hut of some kind has been built. A
sweet smell comes from that direction and you notice the fields of winegrapes
that stretch out around the hut. The air here is calm and not many sounds can be
heard, only from the forest the animals make themself known.
name: Grassy Fields by a Hut

desc:
You enter a part of an ancient forest here, large trees make it hard to see what
is further away in any direction except east, where you see plains stretching
out. Animal sounds echo around you, but you have a hard time spotting any
wildlife. Old boulders and rocks are covered with moss and they look almost like
old trolls that turned into stone, standing forever frozen in one place.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
Suddenly you enter a part of the forest where the sun can filter through the
canopy. The air is still very humid and the moss covers almost everything that
it can cling to. When the sun comes out it shines in rays down on the forest
floor where different kinds of orchids grows, protected from the cold and the
wind by the forest itself.
name: Deep inside an Ancient Forest

desc:
Looking south you see a track going around this part of the forest, it comes up
again to the west. East of you the trees grow dense and tall, just as north of
here. Further into the forest the trees grow very tall and seem to be ancient.
Moss covers the vegetation here and the air is humid, almost wet. The trees
shelter you from any wind and they also reverberate any sound you make.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
All around fields and plains stretch out. The ground is almost bare of denser
vegetation and only a few small trees are visible. Far to the west you can see
the outline of a forest while nothing else of interest is visible in the other
directions.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Small bushes grow here in what seems to be old farmland. Perhaps someone used to
grow crops here but it is evident that the plains have been left to nature a
long time ago. To the northwest you can see a small hut, perhaps that is where
the farmer used to live. North and south of you similar plains stretch out. To
the west you also see more plains, but far to the west you notice a dark line at
the horizon which could mean that there is a big forest there.
name: Bushy Field

desc:
You can see the back of a ruined shack while a large plain stretches out to the
east. To the north the field continues for a bit. A few ruined farm tools lie
against the wall here and peering in through the cracks you see some small
movement inside.
name: Bushy Fields behind a Shack

desc:
Numerous bushes grow here, gradually turning the fields into woodland. To the
west the brushy fields continue while plains can be seen in the east. Looking
north offers a view over the high grass, moving gently in the wind. Southwest of
here is a seemingly abandonded shack. There are a multitude of birds' nests
here.
name: Bushy Fields

desc:
Looking in all directions you see the fields continue. You stand in the middle
of a large grassy field. All around, vast plains stretch towards the horizon.
The high grass is lush and green, making it easy to walk in.
name: Grass Covered Fields

desc:
The grass is only slightly shorter here than to the west. To the south lie
plains looking the same way and to your east there is once again high grass. A
number of large rocks form perfect places for snakes and lizards.
name: Grass Covered Fields

desc:
Several small bushes cover this part of the field. They lack flowers but make it
up with plenty of nasty looking thorns. To the west high grass can be seen, but
to the east the bushy fields continues. North of you grows a shorter kind of
grass which seems to get deeper further north. Looking south you see a ruined
shack that seems to have been the home of a farmer once, now long gone.
name: Bushy Fields

desc:
To the west a trail can be seen, leading north and south. Further east the
fields continue all the way as far as the eye can see. Not a single tree can be
spotted in that direction, but some bushes grow irregularly through the
landscape.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Standing in the high grass with birds flying above your head you see more plains
to the north. West of here is a trail leading north and south, while the grass
looks just as high to the east. The grass is high enough to be the hiding place
of dangerous animals ready to spring upon innocent victims.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The small path ends in front of an old abandoned shack. It seems to have been an
old farm building of some sort, but its state of decay suggests it has been a
while since it was inhabited. Some movement can be seen inside however, though
it is evident that it is an animal moving about. Outside the shack is a dried
out well, with an unusual bucket barely hanging onto a piece of rope. No door is
seen in the entrance - it has simply fallen off the hinges and lies next to the
shack.
name: A Path outside a Ruined Shack

desc:
You enter the small building. Looking around you, you notice a table that is
falling apart and some abandoned kitchen wares. A old bed is standing in the
corner of the room but it looks far from inviting. The wind blows in through
large cracks between the wooden panels and the stove is completely ruined. Some
strange droppings on the floor reveal that a small colony of bats have made this
old building their home.
name: Inside a Small Shack

desc:
North of here you see an old and partly ruined shack. It looks as if it once
belonged to a farmer that used to work these fields a long time ago. The fields
stretch out in all directions and small bushes litter the area. The bushes are
small and most of them have thorns. Some of them might carry berries during the
summer.
name: Bushy Fields by a Shack

desc:
A large and very deep forest is visible south and west of here. Various animal
cries can be heard echoing out between the trees. The wind here is calm and it
smells sweet. To the north you see a small trail, possibly leading to the hut
you can see northeast. The immidiate vincinity is covered with bushes and small
trees.
name: Bushy Field by a Forest

desc:
Travelling westward means that you will go deeper and deeper into this ancient
forest, looking east you see open plains of some sort stretching out. The trees
are growing very tall here and they cover the sky completely with their large,
old and knotted branches. The echoes of animals can be heard coming from deeper
within the forest. You also notice that the wind is cut off by the protection of
the trees, no matter how hard it blows on the plains, here it is always calm.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
If you venture west you will enter a forest that seems to be deep and calm,
while north of you some plains covered with bushes are visible. East and south
of here more fields covered with grass stretch out. The air is humid and still,
the forest must shield this area from the worst winds.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The air is very humid and still here. Not much light can filter down through the
thick branchwork above you. Moss grows on everything and echoes of wild animals
can be heard but you rarely see anything moving. Some boulders are strewn around
the forest floor, many have the shape of living things but are only nature's
work on stone. It is impossible to see far in any direction, the trees simply
grow too thick here. A large boulder looking like a troll is lying here.
name: Deep inside an Ancient Forest

desc:
The trees grow bigger and bigger the deeper into the forest you travel and soon
the sky is hidden from your sight by the large branches sprouting from the
trees. A few birds can be heard calling deep in the forest, their sounds echo
throughout the vicinity. Other living creatures move about, making noises but
you have a hard time spotting any of them. Always present, always distant they
move about in the own business.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
You walk among ancient trees here, so tall that they completely blot out the sky
above you. Boulders covered with moss can be seen here and there, resting as
they have rested undisturbed for eons. Some take on the shape of trolls, some
seem to be frozen forms of different animals. Moss dangles from the limbs of the
trees and a soft fungi smell is hanging in the air. No matter what the weather
is around this spot, the wind will never reach down here.
name: Outskirts of an Ancient Forest

desc:
Rocks litter this area, varying in size, but all very sharp. Travelling is very
awkward here and the trails seen in all directions but to the north must be
easier to travel by. A great oaktree is looming north from here.
name: Rocky Slopes

desc:
This area is completely surrounded by small, rocky hills and other rough
terrain, making travelling here somewhat difficult but not too tiring. A single
large oak tree decorates this area and birds can sometimes be seen flying in and
out of the treetop. A hole in the trunk about halfway up seems to be the home of
a squirrel, based on the litter of nutshells on the ground at the base of the
tree.
name: In the Middle of the Foothills

desc:
This area is with the exception of a few small rocks smooth, making travel a
feat. To the east a trail can be seen, going north and south. Further west a
large tree is growing among the foothills, and to the south you can once again
make out the trail. A gentle foothill stretches north.
name: Smooth Hillside

desc:
A trail can be seen to the west while the foothills stretch out to the north and
east. No trees grow here, but some bushes and rocks litter the area, making
travel somewhat tricky. Rabbits and other small animals has made several tracks
in a small puddle.
name: Among the Foothills

desc:
The foothills offer a nice bit of scenery with several animals darting between
hiding places among the multitude of bushes and rocks. Travelling here is light
although a trail would feel welcome for tired feet. To the north you can see
some plains. The foothills continue in all other directions.
name: Among the Foothills

desc:
Grasses constrict the growth of other plants on the high plains. Few bushes
manage to grow above the grass and even fewer trees manage to reach the sky.
Sounds of wildlife can be heard, but the local flora hides their movement from
sight. The plains continue to the west, but hills surround the field in all
other directions.
name: Lush Grasses

desc:
A few loose rocks make travelling here slightly harder. The local fauna of the
foothills offer no great surprises and the only thing that catches your eye is a
trail far to the east. The air is crisp and fresh.
name: Among the Foothills

desc:
From this smooth hillside you can spot a trail to the east, some smoothly sloped
hills to the north and foothills to the west. Travelling looks slightly harder
in the foothills, albeit it does seem to offer an abundance of wildlife. To the
south more hillsides can be seen.
name: Smooth Hillside

desc:
You are standing on a small round hill, which lies among gently rolling
foothills. To the west you can make out a trail, to the north you see plains and
beyond them, more hills. Looking east you see hills that stretch around to the
southeast. Quite a few bushes make out excellent hiding places for small animals
and birdnests. You spot another trail directly south of here.
name: On a Small Hill

desc:
You have entered a part of the plains that is covered in tall, lush grass.
Northward the grassy plains becomes partly overcome by rocks and boulders of all
sizes. Hills start to form, making the land harder to travel to the south and
several miles to the east as well. Many smaller birds and even an occasional
predator flies about in the air, concentrated to some area west of here.
name: Lush Grasses

desc:
Wild fields of wheat, unattended and overgrown with weeds dominate the landscape
here. Birds are having a paradise. Small vermin scuttle across below the plants
and birds of all sizes and variations fly above. A large monolithic boulder sits
in the centre of the wheat field. The boulder makes an excellent watch point for
large predators that are hunting smaller game.
name: Wild Wheat Field

desc:
A line of limestone streaks across the hillside, a mere twelve feet long and
barely two feet across. Several small rocks have broken off and lie at the base
of the hill. The hills continue to grow and more limestone can be seen to the
north. Vast grasslands lead out of the hills to the west, while smooth hills can
be seen to the east and south.
name: Smoothly Sloped Hills

desc:
Varied bushes and trees dot the hillsides. Signs of wildlife abound. Tracks,
trails, and chewed plants can easily be found. Sounds fill the air. These
smaller hills slope down to a wide plain to the east. Hills rise to new heights
to the northwest.
name: Smoothly Sloped Hills

desc:
Intermittent streaks of limestone break up the grassy hillsides. Several rocks
have split from the rock, tumbling into the gullies. Flowers sprout from the
edges of the torn up ground, where the grass does not choke them off. Thick
brush grows along the base of the hills. The ground rises to the northeast where
a vast grassland begins.
name: Worn Hillside

desc:
Large gaping wounds of limestone streak across the lush grass hills. Deep gashes
have been made by rushing rain waters. Bushes have found the edges favourable
for growth. Game trails meander through the brush, crisscrossing the hill sides.
The hills continue to the east and south, and the land rises to the north.
name: Split Hills

desc:
Many shallow ravines cross the hills, granting passage to the water that has
eroded the hills to the north and west. Lush carpets of grass cover the hills
between the crevasses. Varied flowers have managed to find their place in the
sun. The hills become smoother to the south, which meet with a large plain to
the east.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Various kinds of grass grow in clumps or near larger stones. Weeds are numerous
among the collections of rocks. More plains covered with rocks can be travelled
to the west. East and south the terrain consists of green grassy fields. A
shallow dingle filled with swaying grass lies to the north.
name: Boulder-strewn Grasslands

desc:
Rocks, boulders and stones of all sizes dot these lush grasslands. Moss and
grass partially conceals many of them. Small animal trails lie scattered across
the ground. The rocky fields come to an abrupt end to the northeast. The rolling
plains continue far to the east, south and west. A line of trees indicate the
beginnings of a forest to the northwest.
name: Boulder-strewn Grasslands

desc:
Lush grass grows all around with small area apparently having been grazed upon.
Flowers reach skyward, long stems holding them up towards the sun. Game trails
dart in and out of the many bushes growing upon the plains. A forested areas
begins close to the north and west, while the grasslands continue far to the
south and east.
name: Wild Grasslands

desc:
Long grass covers the area, providing effective cover for the local fauna. Few
trees have been able to take root and survive. One ancient oak spreads it
branches over the plains. The grasses stretch across the ground between a series
of hills to the southeast and a forest to the northwest. Grasslands can be seen
stretching far to the north and south.
name: Wild Grasslands

desc:
Foot high wild grass covers the land for as far as the eye can see. These mainly
flat grasslands continue in all directions except north. A dell between several
hills hides a vast amount of young oak trees to the north. Smaller field animals
roam the grass-covered lands at free will.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
In a shallow depression, weeds and bushes grow in a dense clump. The wind does
not penetrate the thick brush. The occasional limestone rock breaks up the
otherwise flat grasslands. Hilly terrain to the east and south begins a descent
to the coastal plains of the gulf of Luhn. Grasslands continue to the north and
west.
name: A Small Depression

desc:
Deep crevasses cut through the hills, revealing the white limestone lying
beneath the green hills. Trees grow sparsely, but flowers and bushes have found
purchase along the newly exposed ground near the ravines. A lush carpet of grass
covers the plains to the north and west, while a series of hills can be seen to
the south. More hills can be seen to the east.
name: Sharp Hills

desc:
Small crystals create a small amount of light. The swiftly moving underground
stream rushes along, pushing all in its path to the east. Dark cubby holes and
deep crevices line the walls and ceilings, but the water has worn the surface
smooth. There seems to be no place to get a solid grip. A large cavern opens to
the north, but the water wants to go down a chute to the east.
name: Fissure in Blue Limestone

desc:
Ancient diggings line the caverns walls. Bones can be seen lying in many of the
small alcoves. Small pieces of wood and rusted metal sharing their resting
place. A cool mist seeps from the limestone in one corner, where most of the
bones seem to be located. Lichens and moss grow in varied places around the
cavern.
name: Deep in the Glittering Caves

desc:
The inside of the stump is damp and mildewed. Wood crumbles at the slightest
touch, with piles of sawdust pile along the edges already. Hundreds of tiny
holes pierce the inner side of the old tree. In the centre is a small burrow,
which has been dug out by some creature. The outer casing of the trunk leans
against what little remains of a once great oak.
name: Rotting Stump

desc:
Varied molds completely cover the damp rock walls. Water slowly seeps down to
form pools of liquid, which seem to lead deeper into the floor. The air is very
musty, no evidence of a breeze can be felt. Small bones from squirrels or other
rodents can be seen scattered about.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
Most of this cave is underwater and continously fed by waves coming in from the
north. The southern part of the cave slopes up gently until it reaches a dry
rocky plateau. Green algae hang from the ceiling and off of rocky walls. The
only visible exit lies in the north and leads out into the Gulf of Lhun.
name: A Watery Cave

desc:
Salty air reaches here through a slime covered opening to the north while a
tunnel leads off to the south into darkness. Green water runs down from the
ceiling and along the walls making the ground here quite slippery. As waves hit
against the outside of this enclosure, the walls vibrate, sending small sprays
of slime through the air.
name: Tunnel's End

desc:
The air in here is thick and not all of the odours can be attributed to
originating from decaying plant matter. Green slime covers all sections of the
tunnel with the exception of some solid pieces of matter that litter the ground.
Off in the northern distance the rhythmic sounds of waves can be heard as they
crash against obstructions.
name: Bend in Slimy Tunnel

desc:
Bits of grass has been tucked in under mud and slime making this area into a
nest of sorts. It is not hard to tell what has been eaten here, bones litter the
area along with a few smaller skulls. The nest reeks of rat faeces that has
accumulated over many years. The only way to leave here is to the east.
name: A Rat's Nest

desc:
The ceiling, walls and floor of this long tunnel are covered in green slime.
Long trails of slime run down from the ceiling making it difficult to pass
through this tunnel without getting some of it on oneself. Peculiar designs on
the floor suggest that something has recently disturbed the gooey mass. The
tunnel ends abruptly to the south and bends westwards into the darkness.
name: A Slimy Tunnel

desc:
Part of the iron hull of a stove reaches into this room from the kitchen,
providing heat and a place to dry clothes when the stove is in operation. A
wooden table along with three matching chairs stands next to a window by the
northern wall. Shelves filled with pottery and keepsakes line the eastern wall.
The only other furniture in this room is a large rocking chair near the oven.
name: A Living Room

desc:
Much of the ground here is covered by boulders of various sizes. The Gulf of
Lhun lies to the west and it is quite calm in this area. Occasionally larger
waves collide with some of the boulders further out, causing some sprays of
salty water. Little vegetation grows here though some sand is visible amongst
the large rocks. Further east and south the rocks diminish, giving way to more
fertile land.
name: Along a Rocky Beach

desc:
The Gulf of Lhun looks almost inviting as its massive body of grey water
stretches towards the western horizon. The ground here is covered mostly in sand
along with a few hardy bushels of grass. A number of animal tracks can be seen
imprinted in the soft sand. Only a few large boulders remain here, though their
numbers increase in the north.
name: Along a Beach

desc:
Thick branches reach out to the east and west while the main trunk of this oak
continues a short stretch upwards before it too splits into a number of smaller
sections. The branches appear thick, but venturing away from the centre of this
tree might not be a smart idea. The bark of the tree is rough and appears as if
an animal or something has developed a liking for it.
name: Up in a Tree

desc:
The hallway is simply decorated with a worn rug on the wooden floor. There are
doors to the east and west and open doorways north and south. A red candle sits
upon a small cupboard and some dirty shoes have been left on the floor beside
it. Rugged jackets hang on hooks by the southern wall.
name: Entranceway inside a Farmhouse

desc:
The air in this room is substantially colder than the temperature outside it.
Solid rock forms the ground, out of which two steps have been carved to place
the main floor of the room below ground level. A number of large barrels and
boxes occupy the room. Scents of dried meat and rotting vegetables saturate the
stagnant air.
name: A Storage Room

desc:
A large wooden bed takes up most of the space in this room. It is covered with a
number of blankets apparently made from sheep wool. Above the door leading east
hangs a cowhorn stuffed with some dried flowers. Part of the floor is covered
with the skin of a large brown animal. A drawer stands next to a window facing
westwards.
name: Master Bedroom

desc:
Though this room contains no bed, it is apparently used as a bedroom. Bushels of
straw lie by the southern wall and are partially covered by a blanket. The floor
is neither stone nor wood, it is earth that has been pressed flat by many years
of being walked on. A single chair that looks too weak to hold anything, stands
next to a makeshift bed. There are no windows in this room, nor is there a light
source. The only light or airflow here originates from beyond the northern
doorway.
name: A Plain Bedroom

desc:
The sounds of flowing water can be heard directly to the north. This tunnel
would not allow any creature save one the size of a hobbit to walk upright. Some
pieces of earth have come loose from the ceiling resulting in a partial blockage
of the passage. A strange scent reaches you from the south.
name: Near an Opening

desc:
The bloated bodies of some frogs lie scattered in this tunnel, perhaps they were
overcome by the strange smell blanketing this area. From somewhere the sound of
water reaches you. Stagnant air combined with an inexplicable heat, apparently
from decaying organic matter, make this place a great place for the growth of
fungi. The tunnel continues to the west and north.
name: A Low Tunnel

desc:
A few larger pieces of earth have fallen to the ground here from the ceiling but
travel remains relatively unobstructed. The tunnel consists mostly of a hollowed
out earth passage that bends occasionally to avoid large boulders that are
imbedded in the ground. Somewhere to the north the sound of running water can be
heard while south of here the tunnel continues.
name: A Low Tunnel

desc:
A few dried out bits of seaweed suggest that at times this tunnel must be
flooded with water. Small heaps of a dark mushy substance have piled up in the
middle of the tunnel giving off a strong odour. The tunnel leads from here to
the east and to the south where it seems to end.
name: Bend in a Low Tunnel

desc:
The walls and floor surrounding you are covered with some sort of dark sticky
substance that gives off a powerful odour. Discoloured water drips from the
ceiling and forms yellow puddles on the soggy ground. High above you there seems
to be an opening, if you dare climb these walls. A low tunnel that must have
been dug many years ago leads north.
name: Within a Strange Chamber

desc:
This is a large pretty garden, seemingly moonlit even in the light of day. Pale
white flowers tell you something sad but beautiful, and masterly grown trees
offer shadow when the sun is too hot.
name: Starlight Garden

desc:
This tunnel is the natural result of water flowing through here for many years
and digging its way into the earth. The tunnel is surprisingly deep and though
it flows at a high rate here, below in the darkness the water looks calmer.
North the tunnel ends and slopes up gradually to meet open water. The current
keeps pushing you out of the tunnel southwards into what must be the
continuation of a river.
name: A Flooded Tunnel

desc:
Directly above you some logs have been tied together forming a crude bridge. The
water flows quickly here and increases even more towards the south. Tall trees
rise up to the east beyond the rivers edge. A narrow tunnel, totally engulfed in
water is visible northwards. The slopes to the east and west are soft and with
the ends of the bridge placed upon them, climbing up either side is impossible.
name: Beneath a Small Bridge

desc:
A layer of opaque material covers this entire enclosure. Occasionally stems of
waterweeds are visible that have been weaved into the cocoon. Rocky walls can be
seen through a small section of thinner material, the rest of the outside world
is hidden by the silky webbing. The air here is thick and smells strangely.
There is a single exit through a small opening downwards and west, out of the
chamber. A number of bones wrapped in a silky package hang on the sides of the
waterproof enclosure.
name: A Cocoon

desc:
You are in a grassy field that seems to stretch out to the east. North of you
someone is growing some kind of plant in straight rows. To the west a trail can
be seen going south and westward. Small bushes make homes for several kinds of
birds here and holes in the ground give shelter to other animals.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The trail makes an abrupt turn from the south to west here. To the north you see
an immense field stretching out, and beyond that you can see the southern peaks
of Ered Luin. To the southwest you can see a fast flowing river making its way
to the Gulf of Lhun. Down the trail to the south you can make out Mithlond.
name: A Dusty Trail

desc:
The portico of this Elven home looks out onto a vibrant, green lawn speckled
with silvery-white flowers. A set of rickety steps on the western side of the
portico leads towards some shrubbery; a sturdier set of steps leads southwards
to the path through the front lawn. The steps and floor of the portico are made
of ash wood that emits a brilliant shine on cloudless nights and a paler, warmer
glow during the day. The intricate railings and roof supports are made of a
metal sturdier than iron, but they shine silver with the light of many stars.
Strands of ivy weave themselves around the railings and the bottom edges of the
portico so that the house seems to be growing from the ground itself. To the
north is a door leading to the inside of the home.
name: An Ivy Covered Portico

desc:
This room serves both as a bedroom and a private study for Hawa Jadestone, the
mistress of the house. Each wall, except the one containing the door, holds a
large, curtained window. These three magnificent curtains stretch from the
rafters of the ceiling all the way to the floor and serve as eloquent reminders
of far-off Elven cities. The curtains give the room a quiet aura of tranquility
infused with sadness about wonders lost. The walls and floor are made of a
marble very similar in colour to the wood of the portico and glisten when
moonlight makes its way through the curtained windows. Tucked away in one corner
of the room is a wooden chest that contains various pieces of jade jewellery as
well as other trinkets from times gone by.
name: The Hall of Remembrance

desc:
This rooftop terrace has been constructed entirely of white and silver
materials. The perimeter of the terrace is marked by a short, white railing that
serves not only as a precautionary measure, but also as a trellis for radiant
honeysuckle to grow on during the warmer months. The floor is covered by a layer
of pale, flat stones that seem to catch moonlight and harness its brilliance so
that the stones radiate their own light even during the day. In the centre of
the terrace stands a white crescent-shaped fountain that seems specially
designed to capture moonlight. Light reflected off the water within the fountain
plays over the floor in shining waves.
name: A Moonlit Terrace

desc:
The river passes just west of a large island. It flows lazily to the south, from
the north. The water is not very deep here, and you can spot some fishes lurking
around at the pebble covered bottom.
name: River Lhun

desc:
This side channel of the Lhun flows lazily southward; to the east is a long
island which separates it from the vast main channel. South of here the waters
rejoin. To the west is a high earthen bank, too steep to climb; at its top you
can see great leafy trees whose roots are naked at the top of the bank, ready to
be washed away when the next flood undercuts the bank a few feet more.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are near the west bank of a mighty river. The water flows calmly here, but
its power is great. To the south is a great hill, topped by tall pines among
which you can see the grey stones of a ruined building. The river's waters have
undercut the side of the hill, carving a great undercut cliff; in some future
flood, trees, ruins, and all will collapse and be washed away, and eventually
the river will proceed southward unobstructed as it wishes.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Open water surrounds you in all directions yet the sound of crashing waves in
the far east suggest the presence of land. Little seaweed is present, likely due
to the ferocity of the current here. South the water appears to be somewhat
calmer.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
Large waves roll in from the west and flow eastward exposing their white tops.
The water surface is almost black here and shields the bottom of the sea from
view. Sounds of the waves from all directions block out virtually all other
noise.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
Dark open water reaches out in all directions, seemingly endless. Though the
waves here are not particularly frequent, they are strong and high causing you
to be pushed eastward. Occasionally small fish break the surface and are quickly
swallowed up again by the great dark waters.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
Even though the bay is wide here one can recognise certain signs of approaching
inhabited lands. Far to the west one can barely make out the distinct outline of
the lighthouse of Forlond with its beckoning light. Harlond should be some
distance to the south. Here and there the horizon is dotted with the outlines of
elven-made sails.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
Out here in the deep waters one can barely discern the shores of the river and
only the most prominent landmarks are visible above the horizon. There is a
faint current in the water emanating from the Lhun to the north. Fortunately the
sea is not open enough for the winds to create waves that could turn a boat
over. Upon closer examination this area would be the perfect spot for fishing
provided that the season and the weather was the right.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
This is where the clear water of the Lhun yields to the salty ocean. The water
here has become yellow and fast currents whips up large amounts of thick foam.
South of there the currents diminish while the river north is harder to travel.
There is a strong smell of seaweed in the air.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
Broad and mighty here, the great river flows restlessly south towards the sea.
The current here does not prevent the traveller to journey upstream yet to
follow the river southwards is much easier. The waters here are known to be fair
fishing-waters and the sails of the elven fishing-boats that here and there can
be seen at the horizon indicates that the river now approaches the elven-lands
around the Grey Havens.
name: The Gulf of Lhun

desc:
At the beach west of here one can see some unusually old and tall Willow-trees
towering at the edge of the river. Close to the beach the water is stale and
yellow but only a few feet farther out flows the main stream of the river. It is
possible to go north of here, against the current, but much easier is going with
the current to the south.
name: The Great River Lhun

desc:
The western shore of the river lies west of here but to enter the wood that line
the horizon there seems impossible due to a steep cliff that effectively blocks
the way. North, South and East of here the great Lhun runs on and on towards its
ultimate goal: the great ocean.
name: The Great River Lhun

desc:
The river continues here north and south, and you hope that it stays this placid
the rest of your journey. You can go north or south, or east to another part of
the river to the worn trail.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows smoothly along here, and you wonder for how long you will be
able to take advantage of such serene surroundings. You can go back up north, or
you can continue on the river south.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows quietly along here, and you feel at peace in the placid
surroundings. You can keep flowing along the river south, or you can go to
another part of the river east. Further east, you see the worn trail which leads
to the humble lands of the Dwarves.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Directly to the east stands a wooden dock allowing large boats to be tied up to
shelter safely from the sea. Due to your level in the water it would be
impossible for you to do the same here and leave the water via the dock. The
water is quite calm here and the ocean opens up to the west, while coastal
waters continue northeast. A hollow in the rocks to the south might provide a
safe landing.
name: Near a Dock

desc:
The sand here nearly reaches the gulf but there still remains a small stretch of
beach to the west before the waves engulf the land. A fairly large wooden
structure borders the region to the east with a curtain covered window facing
towards the open waters. Solid land continues both towards the north and south.
name: Sandy Beach

desc:
A window is set on the eastern wall providing view of the courtyard outside.
Below the window a rock has been hewed creating a flat work surface. A number of
barrels and jars stand by the southern wall apparently containing a variety of
preservatives. Small wood chunks are piled by the northern wall next to a black
stove.
name: A Kitchen

desc:
This white marble house's small and simple appearance belies its surprising
elegance, warmth and comfort. The front door is adorned with an exquisite
carving of a Mallorn tree, and behind it a large table and several chairs sit in
the centre of the main room. Above the fireplace's mantel hangs an ancient sword
of Elven make; its blade probably still sharp. Other features include several
furs and carpets lining the floor, and a few green tapestries on the walls.
Through the windows, a small garden can be seen. Smaller rooms include a modest
kitchen and a few guest rooms with soft beds for welcoming friends and visitors.
name: Small but Elegant Home

desc:
A small but comfortable-looking hideout has been skilfully constructed on the
top of a tall tree. The walls, roof and door are all made of living branches
making the room to blend perfectly into its surroundings. A splendid view opens
up from the tree, one can see the roofs and walls of the elven city of Harlond,
its famous lighthouse and the Gulf of Lhun itself. The hideout has serene
athmosphere, only seagulls and wind are making occasional, faint noises that
echo in the air.
name: Hvezdicka's Hideout

desc:
This is the home of the farmers that tend to the fields outside. Many diff-
erent tools adorn the toolracks here. Looking around you, you notice that the
hut is actually quite large inside, there is room for a winegrape mill and
tapping equipment. The farmers who work here must live somewhere else and only
come here to work the fields and turn the winebottles. If there is any- one in
you might be able to buy a bottle or two at a reasonable price.
name: Winemaker's Hut

desc:
You enter the cellar of the hut. This cellar is primarily used to store
wine-bottles before they are ready to be sold and to let the wine age and set
properly. It is a large room filled with the sweet scent of wine and the
stronger scent of alcohol. Racks adorn the wall and bottles lie resting in them.
This is the last stage for the wine, before this the wine must mature in large
barrels and those are located just underneath the wooden stairs. A large and
soft spider web covers many of the bottles.
name: In a Mouldy Cellar

desc:
Much of the inside of the oak has been hollowed out at this level, without
killing the tree or disrupting its growth. A number of feathers along with bits
of grass and leaves line the bottom of the small chamber. There appears to be a
tiny opening further up inside the oak that leads outwards, but it is very
narrow.
name: Within an Old Oak

desc:
This cavern must exist deep within the earth for its walls are made out of hard
rock. Though above you the water created a tunnel naturally, down here the
cavern has somehow been hollowed out by tools. The water flow is almost non
existent in complete contrast to the water rushing passed overhead. What could
be a chamber lies to the east, but a strange bubble covers the entrance.
name: A Water Filled Cavern
